Report Editing Basics 
Various report editing functions are available. 
Manually Update Report Output 
It is sometimes necessary to change a small number of values in the report before printing. One way to make 
these changes can be to export the report to an editable file (for example, an RTF file), then use an appropriate 
editor tool to edit values and then print the report. 
To export a report, on the Report Designer tab, in the AVEVA group, click Run Report to display the Run Report 
window with the Print Preview tab displayed. The Export options become available. 
Select Report Controls 
Selects controls for modification. 
Click the left-mouse button to select a control. The selected control is highlighted and can be modified. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4773
To modify multiple controls, use one of the following methods: 
• Select controls while holding Ctrl or Shift. 
• Click on the report blank space and drag the mouse to create a selection frame around the controls. When 
the mouse button is released, all controls within the selection frame boundary are selected. Any previous 
selection is cleared. 
Move Report Controls 
Moves the currently selected control using a drag and drop operation. 
Alternatively controls can be moved using the arrow keys. 
1. Select the control. 
2. Press the left-mouse button and move the mouse to specify the new position of the control. 
Alternatively controls can be moved using <Shift+Arrow> or <Ctrl+Shift+Arrow>. 
3. Release the left-mouse button to move the control. 
Edit Report Controls 
Moves or copies the currently selected control to the pasteboard. Copies the currently selected control from the 
pasteboard. Undoes or redoes the last action. 
On the Report Designer tab, in the Edit group, the following functions are available: 
• Cut 
• Copy 
• Paste 
• Undo 
• Redo 
Modify Report Control Fonts and Colours 
Modifies the font and colour of the currently selected control. 
On the Report Designer tab, in the Font group, the following functions are available: 
• Modify font 
• Background Colour 
• Foreground colour (text colour) 
• Font size 
• Bold 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4774
• Italic 
• Underline 
• Align text left 
• Align text centre 
• Align text right 
• Justify text 
On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab to display various control options. For example, padding 
values, border style and colour. Refer to Styles and Conditional Formatting for further information. 
Functionality is available to customise the appearance of controls. 
Align Report Controls 
Aligns selected controls in relation to other selected controls. 
On the Report Designer tab, in the Alignment group, the following functions are available: 
• Align to grid 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4775
• Align left 
• Align centre 
• Align right 
• Align Top 
• Align middle 
• Align bottom 
Resize Report Controls 
Resizes a control using a drag and drop operation. 
1. Select the control. 
2. Hover the mouse over one of the small boxes on the edge of the control to display an arrow. 
3. Press the left-mouse button and move the mouse to specify the new position of the control. 
Alternatively controls can be resized using Shift+Arrow or Ctrl+Shift+Arrow. 
4. Release the left-mouse button to resize the control. 
Functionality is also available to resize selected controls in relation to other selected controls. On the Report 
Designer tab, in the Layout group, the following functions are available: 
• Size to grid 
• Make same width 
• Make same height 
• Make same size 
• Make horizontal spacing equal 
• Increase horizontal spacing 
• Decrease horizontal spacing 
• Remove horizontal spacing 
• Make vertical spacing equal 
• Increase vertical spacing 
• Decrease vertical spacing 
• Remove vertical spacing 
• Center horizontally 
• Center vertically 
• Bring to front 
• Send to back 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4776
Zoom 
Adjusts the zoom magnification for a report, providing easier control over the report layout. 
On the Report Designer tab, in the Zoom group, the following functions are available: 
• Zoom out 
• Set zoom magnification 
• Zoom in 
Delete Report Control 
Deletes a control. 
Select the control in the report or the Report Explorer window, and press the Delete key. To select multiple 
controls, hold down the <Shift> key while selecting multiple controls. 
Alternatively, right-click the currently selected control, and select Delete from the drop-down menu. 
Note: 
To undo a deletion press <CTRL+Z>. 
Display Values from a Database (Bind Report Controls to Data) 
Report controls can display dynamic data from a bound database. For example, an employee list report may 
need to display birth dates. If a database contains this information, it can easily be added to the report using one 
of the methods described below. 
A databound control is indicated by a yellow database icon in the top-right corner. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4777
Field List 
Creates a control and binds it to database data using the Field List window. 
1. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item. 
2. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the report. A control is created which displays the name of the 
field item and the yellow database icon. 
A field item can also be dragged and dropped from the Field List window onto an existing control to modify the 
content of the control. 
Smart Tag 
Binds an existing control to database data using the Smart tag functionality. 
1. Click the control on the report to be bound to database data. 
2. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top-right of the control to display the Tasks window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4778
3. Select a field item from the Data Binding drop-down list. The control displays the name of the field item and 
the yellow database icon. 
Properties Window 
Binds an existing control to database data using the Properties window. 
1. Click the control on the report to be bound to database data. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Data tab, expand the Text property and select a field item from the 
Binding drop-down list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4779
3. The control displays the name of the field item and the yellow database icon. 
Mail Merge Report Controls 
Several controls support the Mail Merge feature. To use this feature, insert field names surrounded by square 
brackets into the control text. 
Modify Report Control Format 
Modifies the format of the control using a predefined format style. The example describes how to modify the 
numbering format from two decimal places to a single decimal place. 
1. Click the control on the report to be modified. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4780
2. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top-right of the control to display the Tasks window. 
3. Click the ellipsis next to Format String to display the Format String Editor window. 
4. Select the required format style and click OK to close the Format String Editor window and save the changes. 
5. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the modified report control. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4781
Sort Report Data 
Sorts report data using a label control that is bound to a field item. The field item is used as the sorting criteria. 
1. Click the Detail band on the report. 
2. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top of the band to display the Tasks window. 
3. Click the ellipsis next to Sort Fields to display the Group Field Collection Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4782
4. Click Add to add a new entry. 
Note: 
Data can be sorted against multiple field items. If this is required, add more entries and specify the field 
items and sort order settings. 
5. Click OK to apply the settings and close the Group Field Collection Editor window. 
6. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the sorted report data. 
Filter Report Data 
Filters report data using the Properties window. 
1. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4783
3. Click the ellipsis next to Filter String to display the FilterString Editor window. 
4. Use the functionality available to create a filter condition. Multiple filter conditions can be used. 
5. Click OK to apply the settings and close the FilterString Editor window. 
6. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the filtered report data. 
Functionality is available to request a filtering parameter each time a report is previewed. Refer to Parameterised 
Report for further information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting

Page 4784
Group Report Data 
Groups report data using a GroupHeader band with a defined field item which is used as the grouping criteria. 
Note: 
Data grouping can only be performed if a report is bound to a data source. 
1. Right-click anywhere on the report, select Insert Band and GroupHeader from the drop-down menus. 
2. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item to be used as the grouping 
criteria. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4785
3. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the GroupHeader area of the report. A control is created which 
displays the name of the field item and the yellow database icon. 
4. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item to be used as the report 
general data. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4786
5. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the Detail area of the report. A control is created which displays 
the name of the field item and the yellow database icon. 
6. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top of the GroupHeader band to display the Tasks window. 
7. Click the ellipsis next to Group Fields to display the GroupField Collection Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4787
8. Click Add to add a new entry. 
9. Set the Field Name property to the required field. 
10. Set the Sort Order property (ascending or descending). 
11. Click OK to apply the settings and close the Group Field Collection Editor window. 
12. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top of the GroupHeader band to display the Tasks window. 
13. Click the ellipsis next to Group Union and select Whole Page from the drop-down list to start a report group 
on a new page if it does not fit entirely on the previous page. 
14. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the grouped report data. 
Add Report Totals 
Calculates summaries (totals) in a report. 
1. If the Report Footer band is not already present in the report, it must be added. Right-click anywhere on the 
report, select Insert Band and ReportFooter from the drop-down menus. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4788
2. On the Detail area of the report, select the field item for which a summary is to be calculated. Hold down 
Ctrl and drag the field item onto the ReportFooter area of the report to create a copy which will display the 
summary. 
Note: 
A new label can also be created for the summary by dragging it from the Toolbox. 
3. On the ReportFooter area of the report, select the field item. On the Properties window, click the Database 
tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4789
4. Set the Running property to define the calculated values. The available values are Report, Page and Group. 
The default value is Report which calculates the value using all instances of the specified data field. 
5. Set the Function property to define the type of calculation used. 
6. Set the Ignore Null Values and Treat Strings as Numerics values using the check boxes. 
Note: 
The Ignore Null Values option does not affect the result in this example, since by default NULL values are 
treated as if they were zeros. So the Sum is not affected whether or not these values are taken into account. 
This option applies to functions like Count or Average, because it affects the number of controls counted. 
7. Click the ellipsis next to Format String to display the Format String Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4790
8. Select the required format style and click OK to close the Format String Editor window and save the changes. 
9. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the modified report control.

Watermarks are only visible in Preview and HTML View modes. 
Note: 
Watermarks are not included when a report is exported to HTML format. 
1. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab.

Add Page Numbers and System Information 
Adds page numbers or other system information to a report. For example, the current date and time or a user 
name. 
Generally, this information is displayed within the Page Header, Page Footer or Page Margin bands. 
Add Page Numbers 
Adds page numbers to a report. 
1. On the Toolbox, select Page Info.

the current page number (Latin or Roman, uppercase or lowercase) or the current page number and the of 
total number of pages. 
5. Set the Page Number property. 
6. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the modified report control.

Adds the system date and time to a report.

1. On the Toolbox, select Page Info. 
2. Within the required band, click on the report blank space to position the control.

4. Select Current Date and Time from the Page Information drop-down list. 
5. On the Properties window, click the Data tab.

6. Click the ellipsis next to Text Format String to display the Format String Editor window.

2. Within the required band, click on the report blank space to position the control.

Add Calculated Fields 
Performs calculations using data in multiple data fields. 
It is recommended that all fields based on expressions are given a description in the database view which 
includes the expression used. This makes it clear that an expression has been used to calculate the data, and 
what that expression is. This approach is followed in the supplied sample views. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4799
Note: 
Summary field results cannot be used in calculated fields. 
1. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item. 
2. Right-click and select Add Calculated Field from the drop-down menu. The calculated field is added to the 
field list. 
3. On the Field List window, click the created field to display the field properties on the Properties window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4800
4. Click the ellipsis next to Expression to display the Expression Editor window. 
Click Fields to display the field list. Double-click field names to add them to the expression string. Use the 
toolbar to add operators between field names. 
Two custom functions are available to support the extraction of values, units or dimensions from a unit 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4801
qualified attribute returned from string. These functions are: 
Note: 
Calculated fields are not included in the list of fields in the Expression Editor window. Input the name of a 
calculated field into the expression to use the result of that calculated field in that expression. 
Two custom functions are available to support the extraction of values, units or dimensions from a unit 
qualified attribute returned from string. These functions are: 
GetDouble([FieldName]) 
Returns the value of a unit qualified value. 
GetUnits([FieldName]) 
Returns the unit. 
GetDimension([Attribute name]) 
Returns the dimension. 
The function FormatDouble([attribute], ‘units’, precision, 'label', fraction) can be used in a calculated field to 
convert the value of a real attribute from current units to given units and precision. Label and fraction 
parameters are optional where label is a string for overriding the default unit label, and fraction is a boolean 
value to indicate whether a decimal or fractional value for the unit is displayed. For example, the following 
converts HBOR to millimeters with two decimal places, regardless of the units and precision of the current 
session. 
FormatDouble([HBOR], ‘millimeter’, 2) 
FormatDouble([HBOR], 'inch', 2, 'in', true) converts the HBOR to inches with two decimal places 
and displays the inches value with the unit label 'in' and fraction. 
Quick reports created from Tags, where the units of a real attribute can be modified are displayed in the 
same units by creating calculated fields for each real attribute using this function. 
Please note: 
• These functions are not available from the list of functions available in the expression builder, and must 
be manually typed into the expression. The function name should be used followed by brackets. 
• GetDouble, GetUnits and GetDimension do not work in conjunction with other functions. For example 
GetDouble([HBOR]) + [NUMB] or GetDouble([HBOR]) + GetDouble([HBOR]) does not work. A calculated 
field must contain only one of the above functions as an expression. 
Whenever the calculated field uses the attribute name having ] in the attribute name, use the escape 
sequence \] for correct calculation of the expression. 
For example, to get the length of attribute named test pressure [1], use the expression Len([test 
pressure[1\]]). 
This also applies to GetDouble, GetUnits, GetDimension and FormatDouble functions. 
In addition, the following Summary Functions are available for use with unit qualified attributes: 
• Min 
• Max 
• Avg 
• Sum 
These are available in the expression builder, and when setting the summary function for a form control 
bound to a field. 
5. Click OK to save the expression and close the Expression Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4802
6. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the report. A control is created which displays the name of the 
field item and the yellow database icon. 
7. Click the Print Preview tab to display a preview of the current report layout with the created control. 
Functionality is available to perform calculations within a single data field. Refer to Add Report Totals for further 
information. 
Add Arrays 
Reporting does not support the dragging and dropping of an Array field from the Reporting Field List. 
To bind a report item to an Array, place the item on the report, then set the Data Binding property of the control 
to that of the Array field. 
For example: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4803
Add Project Data 
Project data can be added to a report. Generally such information is placed in the Header and Footer bands. 
1. On the Toolbox, select Project Info. 
2. Within the required band, click on the report blank space to position the control. 
Create Reports 
Various report creation functions are available. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4804
Units of Measure 
Defines the default units of measure for the report. 
Units of measure are determined by the Measure Units property. The defined value is used as the basic unit of 
measure for all unit-related report properties (for example, location, size, border and width). The default value is 
Hundredths of an inch. 
1. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Behavior tab. 
3. Select a unit of measure from the Measure Units drop-down list. 
Page Settings 
Defines the page settings for the report. 
In Reporting, the report page settings can be defined in one of two ways. The page settings can either use the 
default printer settings when the report is printed, or use report-specific printer settings. 
Default Printer Settings 
The page orientation and paper size can be set to use printer settings instead of the report specific settings. This 
is useful for reports that are printed in several places using different printers and printer settings. 
1. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Miscellaneous tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4805
3. Expand the Using Settings of the Default Printer property. 
4. Select the Use Landscape check box to use the landscape setting from the default printer. The Landscape 
check box at the top of the Properties window becomes inactive. 
5. Select the Use Paper Kind check box to use the page size setting from the default printer. The Use Paper 
Kind property displayed at the top of the Properties window becomes inactive. 
Report Specific Page Settings 
The page orientation and margins can be explicitly defined for the report. The margin values are displayed using 
the default unit of measure. Predefined paper sizes or printer paper sizes can be selected, custom paper sizes 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4806
can also be created. 
1. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Miscellaneous tab. 
3. Modify the Margins explicitly. 
4. Select a predefined page size from the Paper Kind drop-down list. 
Alternatively, select Custom from the Paper Kind drop-down list, the Page Height, Page Width and Paper 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4807
Name fields become active. 
Alternatively, select Custom from the Paper Kind drop-down list, select a printer from the Printer Name
drop-down list and select a page size associated with the printer from the Paper Name drop-down list. 
The modified settings affect the layout of the report design area. After modification, controls positioned 
outside of the page margins are displayed red. 
Run Report Window Page Setup 
The page setup can be modified using the Run Report window. 
1. On the Run Report window, click the Print Preview tab. 
2. Click the Page Setup launcher to display the Page Setup window. 
The functionality available can be used to adjust the page layout before the report is printed or exported. 
The margins can also be set by dragging the dashed lines on the Run Report window.

Bind Report to Dabacon Project Data 
Binds a report to project data, enabling the data to be used in the report, there are three options available. 
Define New Data Source 
Binds a report to a new data source. 
1. On the Report Designer tab, in the AVEVA group, click Data Source to display the Data Source Wizard. 
2. Select New to define a new data source and click Next. 
3. Input the name and description for the data source and click Next. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4809
4. Select the element types to be included in the report. 
At least one element type must be selected. The selected element type is used to populate the report. 
If required, enter a value in the filter field to filter a list of elements. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4810
5. Select the required elements. Use the Ctrl and Shift keys to make multiple selections. Use the arrow buttons 
to move the currently selected elements in the Available Element Types grid to the Base Element Types 
grid. 
6. Click Next. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4811
Filters can be added to limit the elements retuned by the data source. These filters can be simple attribute 
based comparisons, or more complex PML1 expressions. 
Attribute based comparison filters must be entered in the Attribute Filters field, PML1 expression filters must 
be entered in the Expression Filters field. 
7. If required, click the appropriate Add Filter button and enter or select the filter details in the row of fields 
that is displayed. Multiple filters can be defined. 
8. If required, click Delete to delete the currently selected filter. 
9. Click Next. 
The attributes of the base elements of the data source are displayed in the Available Columns field. 
If multiple elements have been selected for the data source, select All Attributes to display all attributes of 
the element types, or click Common Attributes to display only those attributes common to all the selected 
element types in the Available Columns grid. 
10. Select the required columns to display in the report. Use the Ctrl and Shift keys to make multiple selections. 
Use the arrow buttons to move the currently selected columns in the Available Columns grid to the Data 
Source Columns grid. 
Once one or more columns have been selected, PML1 expressions can be entered into the Data Source 
Columns grid, the results are available for use in the report. Intelligent text can be entered into the 
Definition column, this functionality is only available for use with intelligent tables. For example: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4812
11. If required, click Back to modify the defined data. 
12. Click Finish when the data is defined. 
The new data source is now available for use in the report. For example: 
Copy Data Source 
Creates a new data source as a copy of an existing data source, the copied data source can then be modified, if 
required. 
1. On the Report Designer tab, in the AVEVA group, click Data Source to display the Data Source Wizard. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4813
2. Select Copy to define a new data source as a copy of an existing data source and click Next. 
3. Select a definition set from the View Definition Set drop-down list. 
The existing data sources are listed. Data sources come from three sources. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4814
Element Views 
Element views are created automatically by the system. One element view is created for every base element 
type. Selecting an element view is an easy way to include data from one type of element in a report. 
Project Views 
Project views are stored in a LEXICON dictionary database. Read access to the LEXICON database is required 
to see Project Views. Project Views can contain more complex data, and are set up by an administrator. A set 
of sample project views are included with the sample project. 
User Views 
User views can be copies of Project views or Element views, or data sources created and stored locally. 
A copy of the chosen view is taken for the report, the original view is not modified. Write access to important 
project resources is not required. For example, the LEXICON dictionary database. 
4. If required, filter the displayed views based on the owning database type of the elements. 
View sets are used to group views together. This is used purely to help the administrator to group logically 
related views together, for ease of distribution and use. 
5. Select the data source to be copied and click Next. 
The subsequent steps of the Data Source Wizard are identical to defining a new data source, the data source 
details are already populated and can be modified if required. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4815
Edit Data Source 
Modifies the data source of the current report. 
Creates a new data source as a copy of an existing data source, the copied data source can then be modified, if 
required. 
1. On the Report Designer tab, in the AVEVA group, click Data Source to display the Data Source Wizard. 
2. Select Edit to modify the data source. 
The subsequent steps of the Data Source Wizard are identical to defining a new data source, the data source 
details are already populated and can be modified if required. 
Define Data Source Scope 
Once a data source has been defined, the elements from within that source can be further restricted by defining 
a data source scope. 
Only the elements within the chosen scope are examined by the data source, and only those that meet the 
selection criteria of the data source are returned in the report. For example, element types or filters. 
1. On the Report Designer tab, in the AVEVA group, click Scope to display the Scope window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4816
The Scope window is used to specify a list of elements. The report is restricted to source data at and below 
the level of the selected elements in the element hierarchy. 
2. Select the CE check box to make the current element permanent within the scope. 
Note: 
If the CE is modified, so is the scope of the data source. 
3. Click Add CE to add the currently selected element on the Model Explorer to the scope. The element is 
added to the Root Elements list. Multiple elements can be added to the Root Elements list. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4817
4. If required, click Remove to remove an the currently selected element from the Root Elements list. 
5. If the scope of the data source is to be restricted to elements either partially within or completely within a 
specified volume, select either Partially Within or Completely Within from the Volume drop-down list to 
display the Volume window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4818
6. Specify the required volume and click OK to return to the Scope window. 
7. Specify whether the scope is to be restricted to elements in the drawlist or in the obstruction list using the 
From drop-down list. Alternatively, leave the drop-down list set to the default selection of MDB, allowing all 
elements in the database to be in the scope. 
8. Click OK to save the scope settings and exit the Scope window. Click Cancel to exit the window without 
saving. 
Note: 
If root elements, added to the scope, are no longer available in the current database, the DbRef is displayed 
using the format "=dbref[0]/dbref[1]".

1. On the Report Designer tab, click 
 at the top-left of the report to display the Report Tasks window.

Report Types 
Various Report Types can be created. 
Static Report 
A static report is a report that is not bound to a data source. The example describes how to create a single page 
announcement which is repeated twenty times in a report. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. On the Toolbox, select a Rich Text control. 
3. Click on the Detail blank space to position the control.

4. Click the Smart tag symbol at the top-right of the control to display the Tasks window.

the RTF or TXT file. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Note: 
Additional text formatting can be performed. Use the functionality available on the Report Designer tab, in 
the Font group.

8. On the Properties window, click the Miscellaneous tab.

Table Report 
Creates a report using table cells that are bound to a data source. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. Bind the report to a data source. 
3. On the Toolbox, select a Table control.

4. Click on the PageHeader blank space to position the control.

header. The table control positioned in the Detail band is used to contain the report detail. 
7. Type values in the table header cells to be used as the table headers. 
8. Drag and drop or use Smart tags to bind the attributes from the Field List tab of the Report Explorer window 
to the table cell controls.

Parent (Master)-Detail Report (Detail Report Bands) 
Creates a parent-detail report (report with hierarchically linked data) using a Detail Report band.

2. Bind the report to a data source containing a data relationship. 
3. Deselect all controls and make sure the report name is displayed at the top of the Properties window. 
4. Right-click and select Insert Detail Report from the drop-down menu. 
If the bound data source contains a data relationship, the submenu contains an item with the name of that

Detail Report bands.

Categories Products section) or the report will return incorrect output.

Chart with Static Series 
Creates a report with a chart control bound to a data source, so that a particular series has its own data source. 
Note: 
In this simple example, the series has a single data source. Different data sources can be used for different series 
if required. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. On the Toolbox, select a Chart control. 
3. Click on the Detail blank space to position the control. 
Note: 
The Chart Wizard may be displayed at this point (if the Show wizard every time a new chart is added option 
is enabled). Click Cancel to close the Chart Wizard and manually customise the chart. 
4. To bind the chart to a data source, click the Smart tag, and select Add New DataSource from the Data 
Source drop-down list. The Report Wizard is displayed. 
The wizard can be used to assign a non-dabacon data source to the chart. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4838
The specified data source is then assigned to the chart's Data Source property. The Data Member property is 
also set, which specifies which table or view of the dataset the chart obtains data from. 
Note: 
The report Data Source property must be set to None. Otherwise, the chart is blank when previewed. 
5. To add a series to the chart and specify the data binding properties, use the Series Collection Editor. It can 
be displayed either via the Properties window, or via the Series link using the Smart tag for the chart control. 
6. In the Series Collection Editor, click Add, select the Bar view and click OK to create a series. 
7. Switch to the Properties tab. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4839
8. Set the Data Source property to the created dataset, define the Argument Data Member and Value Data 
Members properties. 
9. Click Copy to create another series with identical properties, and set the View property to Point. 
10. Click Close to save the changes and close the Series Collection Editor. 
Note: 
After this step, there may be too many data points which makes the chart difficult to interpret. The chart's 
Data Filters property is used to limit the number of data points shown by applying a filtering criterion. Click 
Series1 in the chart to select it, then in the Properties window locate the Data Filters item and click the 
ellipsis to display the Data Filter Collection Editor. 
11. Click Add to add a criterion, and define the properties: 
12. Click Close to save the changes and close the Data Filter Collection Editor. 
13. Repeat the same actions for Series2, for example, select the Data Filters property, add a filter and define the 
Column Name. 
A number of modifications can be made to improve the appearance of a chart, for example: 
• Remove the legend as it displays the same data for each series. Select the legend item on the chart and 
in the Properties window, set the Visibility property to No. 
• The point labels for Series1 are unnecessary. Select the label and set the Labels Visibility property to No. 
• Customise the Series2 marker display. Replace the default circle with an upside-down triangle using the 
View > Point Marker Options> Kind property and set the Size to 12. 
• Rotate the X-axis labels for better readability. Click the AxisX item in the chart and modify the properties 
for the labels using the Label property. For example, set the Angle property to 20 and the Antialiasing 
property to Yes to improve the label display. 
14. Click the Print Preview tab to display the result. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4840
Refer to Create Layout Template / Report Definition and Bind Report to Non-Dabacon Data for further 
information. 
Chart with Dynamic Series 
Creates a report with a Chart control bound to data, all series are auto-created using the series template which 
specifies common data binding properties. 
The data for all series (the series names along with series point arguments) must be stored in the same data 
table. Also, the series view type and certain other settings must be the same for all the created series. 
In this example the GSP table of the sample gsp.mdb database is used. This table contains the Gross State 
Product (GSP) statistics for some US regions. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. On the Toolbox, select a Chart control. 
3. Click on the Detail blank space to position the control. 
Note: 
The Chart Wizard may be displayed at this point (if the Show wizard every time a new chart is added option 
is enabled). Click Cancel to close the Chart Wizard and manually customise the chart. 
4. To bind the chart to a data source, click the Smart tag, and select Add New DataSource from the Data 
Source drop-down list. The Report Wizard is displayed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4841
The wizard can be used assign a data non-dabacon source to the chart. 
The specified data source is then assigned to the chart's Data Source property. The Data Member property is 
also set, which specifies which table or view of the dataset the chart obtains data from, as is the Data 
Adapter property. 
Note: 
The report Data Source property must be set to None. Otherwise, the chart is blank when previewed. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4842
5. To specify the data field which provides data for the series names, set the Series Data Member property. 
6. Set the Argument Data Member property. 
Set the Data Members property, specify the data fields from which the series obtains the data values of its 
points. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4843
A number of modifications can be made to improve the appearance of a chart, for example: 
• Adjust the Series Name Template. By default, the name for every series, automatically generated by the 
chart using the Series Data Member property, is obtained directly from a data field in the bound data 
source. It may be necessary to add prefixes or suffixes to these names. Customise the Series Name 
Template object returned by the Series Name Template property to add some text to the beginning or 
to the end of the series names. For example, set the Series Name Template > Begin Text property to GSP 
in. 
• Customise Series Labels. Set the Series Template > Label Visibility property to No to hide labels for all 
series points and prevent the chart from being crowded with numerous overlapping labels. 
• Customise Axis Labels. Initially, all axis labels (which, in the preview example, display the names of the 
US regions) are lined up and overlap. To avoid this, select the X-Axis (which is also accessed via the Chart 
Control > Diagram > Axis X property) and set the Label.Staggered property to Yes. 
7. Click the Print Preview tab to display the result. 
Refer to Create Layout Template / Report Definition and Bind Report to Non-Dabacon Data for further 
information. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4844
Styles and Conditional Formatting 
The appearance of reports is controlled by styles and conditional formatting. 
Styles Concepts 
Each report control (including report bands) and the report itself, has a group of properties specifying their 
appearance. For example, Background Color, Border Color, Border Dash Style, Border Width, Font, Foreground 
Color, Padding and Text Alignment. By default, these properties are set to no, meaning that the properties are 
obtained from the parent control or band. Appearance-related properties, defined for a parent, are inherited by 
their child controls. The example describes how to create a colour value that is inherited in created child controls 
and how to override the inherited colour. 
1. Click the Detail band on the report. 
2. On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab. 
3. Select Red from the Background Color drop-down list. 
4. On the Toolbox, select a Label control. 
5. Click on the Detail blank space to position the control. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4845
The background colour of the label control displays with a red background colour which is inherited from the 
Detail band. 
6. Click on the label control and on the Properties window, click the Appearance tab. 
7. Select DarkOrange from the Background Color drop-down list to override the inherited background colour. 
8. On the Properties window, click the green square to the right of the Background Colour drop down list and 
select Reset from the drop-down menu. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4846
The background colour of the label control displays with a red background colour which is inherited from the 
Detail band. 
Note: 
Inherited properties are only available for certain controls. For example, the Text Alignment properties of 
Line container bands are ignored and not inherited by Line controls. 
Additionally, styles can be created in a report. Report styles are stored in the Style Sheet collection. A style 
stored within this collection has the same set of appearance properties as a control or a band. 
There are two ways to store report style sheets. The first approach is to save them to external files (using a REPSS 
extension), and then load them into a report using the Style Sheet Path property in read-only mode. The second 
approach is to store the style sheets within the report (using the Style Sheet property), the style sheets can be 
modified and saved with the report itself. 
Finally, styles can be assigned to bands and controls. The display of the band or control can be defined from an 
assigned style, or from the appearance properties. The Style Priority property can be used to define which 
control properties are defined by the associated style. 
By default, all the Style Priority options (for example, Use Background Color and Use Border Color) which follow 
the structure of the style and appearance properties, are set to Yes (except the Use Text Alignment). This means 
that any style that is assigned to a control via the Styles property is a higher priority than the properties stored in 
the control or its parent. If some properties are to be determined by a control, rather than the style, set the 
corresponding Style Priority options to No. 
Note: 
If styles contained in a style sheet loaded using the Style Sheet Path property have the same names as styles 
already contained in a report, the latter styles are overridden. 
The example describes how the Style Priority property works. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4847
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4848
Note: 
When conditional formatting is used, the appearance defined by the conditional formatting has a higher priority 
than the properties described above. 
Another commonly used feature is odd-even styles. These allow the use of alternating data fields in a report, for 
better readability. 
Odd and Even Styles 
Creates alternating data fields in a report for better readability. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. Bind the report to a data source. 
3. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item. 
4. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the Detail blank space of the report. A control is created which 
displays the name of the field item and the yellow database icon. Add multiple field items to the report. 
5. Click the Detail band on the report. 
6. On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab, expand the Styles property and select New from the 
Even Style drop-down list. 
Style1 is applied to the Even Style property. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4849
7. Expand the Even Style property and set the Background Color property to Beige (or any other of the 
available properties in this section as required). 
8. Repeat the previous three steps for the Odd Style property. For example, create a new style and set the 
Background Color property to Ivory. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

9. Click the Print Preview tab to display the result.

Store and Restore Style Sheets 
Stores a report style sheet (containing all the report styles) in an external REPSS file. The report display can be 
restored using the REPSS file. 
To store and restore report style sheets, follow these steps: 
1. Create a new report. 
2. Bind the report to a data source. 
3. On the Report Designer tab, click 
 at the top-left of the report to display the Report Tasks window. 
4. Click the ellipsis next to Style Sheet to display the Styles Editor window. 
5. Click Add a style to create the new styles and define the style properties. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4854
6. Define the style properties and click Save styles to a file to display the Save As window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4855
7. Navigate to the required file location. 
8. Input a filename in the File name field. 
9. Select Report StyleSheet files (*.repss) from the Save as type drop-down list. 
10. Click Save to save the file. 
11. In the Styles Editor window, click Remove a style to delete the created styles. 
12. Click Close to close the Styles Editor window. 
13. On the Report Designer tab, click 
 at the top-left of the report to display the Report Tasks window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4856
14. Click the ellipsis next to Style Sheet to display the Styles Editor window. 
15. Click Load styles from a file to display the Open window. 
16. Navigate to the REPSS file and click Open. 
17. The styles are added to the Styles Editor window and can be used throughout the report. For example, odd 
and even styles can be associated with the Detail band. 
Note: 
The styles loaded from a style sheet file have a higher priority than the styles which exist in a report style 
sheet, returned by the Style Sheet property. This means that if the styles stored in the report have the same 
names as the styles loaded from a style sheet file, the styles in the file are used in the report. 
Refer to Create Layout Template / Report Definition and Bind Report to Dabacon Project Data for further 
information. 
Conditionally Modify Control Display 
Modifies the control display using a condition based rule. 
1. Create a new report. 
2. Bind the report to a data source. 
3. On the Field List window, click the Field List tab and select the required field item.

4. Drag and drop the selected field item onto the Detail blank space of the report. A control is created which

6. On the Properties window, click the Appearance tab. 
7. Click the ellipsis next to Formatting Rule Sheet to display the Formatting Rule Sheet Editor window. 
8. Click Add a formatting rule to create the new rule and define the rule properties.

Page 4859
11. Define the Boolean condition. In the example, the fields are formatted if the [COVX] field is greater than 120.

14. Click the band or control to associate the formatting rule, (in this example, the Detail band).

Cross-references are links, the targets of which are located within the current document and are used to navigate 
to a specific area of a report. 
In the example, a report is created with a link placed on the page footer, linking to the beginning of the report 
from the bottom of each page.

2. Right-click anywhere on the report, select Insert Band and ReportHeader from the drop-down menus.

Page 4881
Miscellaneous 
Various functions which do not fall into other categories are available. 
Handle Events via Scripts 
Reporting includes a scripting feature to handle the events of controls, bands, or reports themselves. 
Overview 
Scripts can be inserted into a report and executed when the report is previewed, printed or exported. Script 
commands must be placed within the event handlers of the report objects. When the corresponding event 
occurs (for example, a mouse click), the script code runs. 
Generally scripts are used for data-aware report formatting. Although Reporting can be used to perform many 
tasks without any scripting, sometimes scripts are required to achieve more specific results. The report layout 
can be fully customised using scripts. 
Scripting is the only way to calculate custom summaries. 
Every report object has a set of events that can result in a script being run. The set of events depends on a 
particular control type. For example, the events for a Label control are: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4882
When a script is selected from a drop-down list for any event, the Script Editor displays. 
Alternatively, on the Report Designer tab, in the Scripts group, click Scripts to display the Script Editor. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4883
If there is no script defined for the event, the Script Editor contains a script template in the language specified by 
the Script Language property of the report (C#, Visual Basic or J#). 
Insert the script code into the Script Editor. 
Click Validate to validate the script code. Errors are displayed in the Script Errors window. Double click on a script 
error to navigate to the error in the script code. 
If there are errors in the script, an error message displays when the report is previewed. 
The scripts are saved along with the report layout to a file. 
Scripting Language 
Report scripts can be written in the following languages that the .NET framework supports: C#, Visual Basic and 
J#. Since J# is not installed with the framework installation by default, make sure it has been installed before 
writing code. The scripting language is specified via the Script Language property of the Report object. C# is the 
default language. 
Scripting Scope 
Script execution is performed in the following way: 
• The report engine generates a temporary class in memory. The names of the variables are defined by the 
Name properties of the controls and objects they represent. When the script is pre-processed, its 
namespace directives are taken from the script code and added to the namespace where the temporary 
class is defined. 
• After pre-processing, all scripts are placed in the code of the temporary class. The resulting class is then 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4884
compiled in memory, and its methods are called when events occur. 
• Scripting offers many advantages: to declare classes (they will become inner classes), variables and methods. 
A variable declared in one script is accessible in another script, because it is in fact a variable of the 
temporary class. 
Reference External Assemblies 
The Script References property of the Report object specifies the full paths (including the file names) to the 
assemblies that are referenced in the scripts used in a report. These paths should be specified for all the 
assemblies that are included in scripts via using (C#), Imports (Visual Basic) or import (J#) directives. 
Note: Assemblies do not usually need including because most standard assemblies that are required in scripts 
are already referenced by Reporting. 
Example: Custom Summary 
In the example, the total number of product unit packs in a group is displayed. 
The steps are similar to the functionality described in Add Report Totals, except that for the summary field, the 
Function property must be set to Custom. 
The additional events must then be added to the label's Scripts property. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4885
Handle the events in the following way:

Smart Tags 
Most controls have Smart tags that provide easy access to the most frequently used settings. Clicking a control’s 
Smart tag displays a popup window with action links and editors for control customisation. 
Report Smart Tag 
A report Smart tag icon 
 is located at the top left corner of the design panel. 
Band Smart Tag 
A band Smart tag icon is located on the band strip right next to the caption. For example, the Smart tag for the 
Detail band is displayed in the following image:

Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4900
Control Smart Tag 
A control Smart tag icon is located in the top right corner of the control. For example, the Smart tag for a Label 
control is displayed in the following image: 
Picture Box Control Smart Tag 
A picture box control Smart tag icon is located in the top right corner of the control. For example, the Smart tag 
for a Picture Box control is displayed in the following image: 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4901
Report Settings 
While the main report properties can be accessed via the report Smart tag, the complete settings list is available 
on the Properties window. The report properties are divided into the following tabs: 
Appearance 
Background Color 
Specifies the background colour of controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Report background colours are not included when a report is exported to RTF or MHT format. 
Border Color, Border Dash Style, Border Width and Borders 
Specifies border settings for controls. 
Font 
Specifies the font settings for controls. Some of the functionality is available on the Report Designer tab. 
Foreground Color 
Specifies the text colour for controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Formatting Rule Sheet 
Displays the Formatting Rule Sheet Editor window, used to manage and customise formatting rules, which can 
then be defined for report bands and controls. 
Formatting Rules 
Displays the Formatting Rules Editor window, used to choose which rules are applied to a report during report 
generation, and to define the priority of the applied rules. 
Padding 
Specifies indent values used to render the contents of the controls. 
Page Color 
Specifies the page background fill colour. 
Style Sheet 
Displays the Styles Editor window, which is used to manage and customise report style sheets. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4902
Style Sheet's Path 
Defines a path to the style sheet which is contained in a report style sheet file (.repss). 
Text Alignment 
Changes the alignment of report control text. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab. 
Watermark 
Customizes the report watermark options. 
Behaviour 
Export Options 
Sets the export options for each file type. For example, PDF, XLS, XLSX, TXT. The options vary with Reporting 311 
file type. 
Horizontal Content Splitting 
Specifies whether report controls outside the bottom page margin are split across pages, or moved in their 
entirety to the next page. 
Measure Units 
Specifies the units of measurement for a report (one hundredth of an inch, a tenth of a millimeter or pixels). 
Request Parameters 
Defines whether the report parameter values are requested when generating a report. 
Script Language 
Specifies the programming language to be used in scripting (C#, Visual Basic or J#). 
Script References 
Manages the collection of strings that represent the paths to the assemblies used by scripts in a report. 
Scripts 
Contains events which can be handled with scripts. 
Show Margin Lines in Preview 
The page margin lines are dotted lines which display on the currently selected page in the report preview. Use 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4903
the property to modify the visibility of margin lines. 
Vertical Content Splitting 
Specifies whether report controls outside the right page margin are split across pages, or moved in their entirety 
to the next page. 
Visible 
Specifies whether the band is visible when using the print preview. 
Data 
Calculated Fields 
Displays the Calculated Field Collection Editor window, which is used to manage the report calculated fields. 
Data Adapter 
Specifies the report data adapter settings, used to populate the report data source. A data adaptor is created 
automatically when the Data Member property is defined. 
Data Member 
Specifies the specific list (database table) in a report data source, from which Reporting objects display data. 
Note: 
It is not usually necessary to specify the Data Member property when binding a report to data. This property 
should only be set if the Data Source dataset contains more than one table. 
Data Source 
Determines the data source that provides the detail data for the report. If this property is not defined, the detail 
report gets data from the primary report data source. 
Filter String 
Displays the Filter String Editor window, which is used to define a filtering condition for report data. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4904
Parameters 
Displays the Parameter Collection Editor window, which provides access to the report parameters collection. 
Tag 
Adds additional information to a report, for example, the id, by which the report can then be accessible via 
scripts. 
XML Data Path 
Used to define a path to data contained in an external XML file. The data contained in the file is used as the 
report data source. 
Miscellaneous 
(Name) 
Determines the name of the report, by which it can be accessed in the Report Explorer, Properties window or 
via scripts. 
Bands 
Displays the Report Editor window, which is used to manage and customise the report bands. 
Bookmark 
Defines how a report is named in a document map when report bookmarks are implemented. The default is the 
Name property of the report. 
Bookmark Duplicate Suppress 
Specifies if duplicated bookmarks are suppressed in the final report. 
Data Source's Schema 
Enables loading of an XML/XSD file, containing the schema of the report data source. 
Designer Options 
Provides access to the hints and warnings during report design. 
Show Designer's Hints 
Designer hints are intended to provide tooltips, both describing the purpose of certain controls for inexperienced 
users, and describing the reasons and solutions for possible export or printing warnings. The property is used to 
turn the hints on or off. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4905
Show Export Warnings 
Controls whether or not export warnings are displayed when report controls are overlapped, indicating that a 
report layout may be incorrect when exporting to certain table-based formats (for example, XLS, XLSX, HTML or 
RTF). 
Show Printing Warnings 
Controls whether printing warnings are displayed. Printing warnings indicate that a report control is placed 
outside the page margin, causing the report to generate unnecessary pages. 
Display Name 
Specifies the name displayed in the report title of another end user’s Designer tab, the display name is also used 
as the default file name for an exported report. 
Draw the Grid 
Determines whether or not the grid is displayed during report design. 
Draw the Watermark 
Specifies the display of a watermark during report design. 
Landscape 
Determines if the page orientation is landscape (when checked) or portrait (when unchecked). 
Margins 
Determines the report margins (in report measurement units). 
The report top and bottom margin properties are linked to the height property of the page margin bands, 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4906
modifying the margin values is reflected in the appropriate band’s height value, and vice versa. 
Page Height 
Specifies the page height in report measurement units. The functionality is only available if the Paper Kind 
property is set to Custom. 
Page Width 
Specifies the page width in report measurement units. The functionality is only available if the Paper Kind 
property is set to Custom. 
Paper Kind 
Determines the type of paper that the report is printed on. Setting this property to any value other than Custom 
prevents the Page Height, Page Width and Paper Name properties from being customised. 
If the Paper Kind property is set to Custom, the printer paper is selected according to the Paper Name property 
value. In this case, it is also necessary to set the Page Width and Page Height properties to the values of the 
selected paper. 
Paper Name 
Determines the name of the custom paper used by the printer on which the report is printed. 
The Paper Name value is applied only when the Paper Kind property is set to Custom. If this property is used, it is 
also necessary to set the Page Width and Page Height properties to the values of the selected paper. If the 
printer on which a document is printed does not support the paper type specified by the Paper Name value, it 
defaults to the Letter paper size. 
Printer Name 
Determines the name of the printer to use when printing a report. 
Report Print Options 
Provides access to options that define the printed report. 
Blank Detail Count 
Specifies the number of times the empty detail band is repeated before printing the regular data. 
Detail Count 
Specifies the maximum number of times the detail band is printed when a data source is defined for the report, 
and it is not empty. 
Detail Count at Design Time 
Specifies the number of times the detail band is printed when a report is being previewed. 
Detail Count when Data Source is Empty 
Specifies the number of times the detail band is printed when no data source is defined for the report. 
Print When Data Source is Empty 
Specifies whether or not a report is printed if the data source is empty. 
Right to Left 
Specifies the content orientation of the controls. 
Right to Left Layout 
Specifies the position of controls with report bands. 
Roll Paper 
Specifies if the report is being printed on roll paper (a continuous sheet). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4907
Show Print Margins Warning 
Specifies whether an error message is displayed when the page margins are set outside the printable area. 
Show Print Status Dialog 
Specifies whether a print status window is displayed when the report is printed. 
Snap Grid Size 
Determines the size of the grid (if displayed) during report design, the grid display is dependent on the Draw the 
Grid property value. 
Snap Grid Step Count 
Specifies the number of grid cells comprising a larger snap grid unit. 
Snapping Mode 
Specifies the snap mode for controls when resized or moved. The following snapping mode are available: Snap to 
Grid or Snap to Lines. 
Using Settings of the Default Printer 
Specifies which of the default printer settings are used when printing a report. 
Favorites 
Right-click on the Favorites tab and select Edit Favorite Property List to display the Favorite Properties Editor, 
which is used to customise the available properties on the Favorites tab. 
Refer to Conditionally Modify Control Display, Store and Restore Style Sheets, Add Watermark, Handle Events via 
Scripts, Bind Report to Dabacon Project Data and Sort Report Data for further information. 
Report Bands 
Reporting creates banded reports. Bands are report sections that can be printed once in a report (report header 
and footer), on each report page (page header and footer), for each data entry (detail band), or for each data 
group (group header and footer). 
Manage Bands 
Report bands can be managed using the Report Editor window. 
Click the report Smart tag icon 
 which is located at the top left corner of the design panel and select Edit and 
Reorder Bands from the Report Tasks drop-down list to display the Report Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4908
Alternatively, click the Smart tag on any band and select Edit and Reorder Bands from the Tasks drop-down list 
to display the Report Editor window. 
Alternatively, right-click anywhere on the report, select Edit and Reorder Bands from the drop-down menu to 
display the Report Editor window. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4909
The Report Editor window can be used to add, remove and reorder the report bands, and to define individual 
properties. 
Detail Band 
The Detail Band is the central part of a report. Unlike other report bands, this band cannot be deleted - the 
report structure includes the detail band as its core. The contents of the detail band are repeated for every data 
entry. For example, if a Tank Capacity Report is processed, a data entry is listed for each valid Space Name. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4910
On the Properties window, the Detail Band properties are divided into the following tabs: 
Appearance 
Background Color 
Specifies the background colour of controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Border Color, Border Dash Style, Border Width and Borders 
Specifies border settings for controls. 
Font 
Specifies the font settings for controls. Some of the functionality is available on the Report Designer tab. 
Foreground Color 
Specifies the text colour for controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Formatting Rules 
Displays the Formatting Rules Editor window, used to choose which rules are applied to a report during report 
generation, and to define the priority of the applied rules. 
Height 
Specifies the height in report measurement units.The default height for a created detail band is 100. 
Padding 
Specifies indent values used to render the contents of the controls. 
Style Priority 
Defines the priority of various styles (for example, background colour or border colour). 
Styles 
Defines odd and even styles for the controls, as well as assigning an existing style to the them (or a newly created 
one). 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4911
Text Alignment 
Changes the alignment of report control text. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab. 
Behaviour 
Drill Down Control 
Specifies a control used to expand/collapse the band. 
Drill Down Expanded 
Specifies if the band is expanded or collapsed when using the drill down functionality. 
Keep Together 
Specifies whether the contents of the band can be horizontally split across pages. Reporting attempts to keep 
sequential detail band entries on a single page. The option applies to data that is grouped or in a parent-detail 
report. If a group does not fit, and would start in the middle of a page, the report moves the group to a new 
page, thus reducing the number of page breaks in continuous data. 
Keep Together with Detail Reports 
When the check box is selected, the detail band is always printed on the same page as the child detail report 
band, or multiple bands. 
Multi-Column Options 
Prints the content of the band using multiple columns. 
Page Break 
Use this property if the current report design requires that the detail section must be separated from previous 
sections or follow-ups. Specify the Before the Band or After the Band values to insert a page break before or 
after the current band. In many cases, this property can be used instead of the Page Break control. 
Scripts 
Contains events which can be handled with scripts. 
Visible 
Specifies whether the band is visible when using the print preview. 
Data 
Sort Fields 
Displays the Group Field Collection Editor, which is used to specify the fields for sorting records in the band, the 
sorting order (Ascending/Descending) for each field and the order sequence. 
Tag 
Adds additional information to a report, for example, the id, by which the report can then be accessible via 
scripts. 
Miscellaneous 
(Name) 
Determines the name of the report, by which it can be accessed in the Report Explorer, Properties window or 
via scripts. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4912
Snap Line Padding 
Specifies indent values used to render the contents of the band, which are maintained when the control is 
aligned using snap lines. 
Sub-Bands 
Displays the Sub-Band Collection Editor window, which is used to manage the report sub-bands. 
Favorites 
Right-click on the Favorites tab and select Edit Favorite Property List to display the Favorite Properties Editor, 
which is used to customise the available properties on the Favorites tab. 
Refer tro Conditionally Modify Control Display, Styles Concepts, Multi-Column Report, Handle Events via Scripts 
and Sort Report Data for further information. 
Group Header and Footer Bands 
The Group Header and Group Footer bands display above and below each group. 
Group Header bands can be used to group multiple data fields in one of the following ways: 
Add several Group Header bands. Specify one group field per band. This results in nested grouping of the report 
data. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4913
Alternatively, specify several group fields for the Group Fields property of the Group Header band. The data is 
grouped by field value combinations. 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4914
The Group Footer band is unnecessary without the corresponding Group Header band. When present, it can be 
customised using a set of properties similar to those of the Group Header. 
On the Properties window, the Group Header/Footer Band properties are divided into the following tabs: 
Appearance 
Background Color 
Specifies the background colour of controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Border Color, Border Dash Style, Border Width and Borders 
Specifies border settings for controls. 
Font 
Specifies the font settings for controls. Some of the functionality is available on the Report Designer tab. 
Foreground Color 
Specifies the text colour for controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Formatting Rules 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4915
Displays the Formatting Rules Editor window, used to choose which rules are applied to a report during report 
generation, and to define the priority of the applied rules. 
Height 
Specifies the height in report measurement units.The default height for a created group header or group footer 
band is 100. 
Padding 
Specifies indent values used to render the contents of the controls. 
Style Priority 
Defines the priority of various styles (for example, background colour or border colour). 
Styles 
Defines odd and even styles for the controls, as well as assigning an existing style to the them (or a newly created 
one). 
Text Alignment 
Changes the alignment of report control text. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab. 
Behaviour 
Drill Down Control 
Specifies a control used to expand/collapse the band. 
Drill Down Expanded 
Specifies if the band is expanded or collapsed when using the drill down functionality. 
Group Fields 
The property is available for the Group Header band only. It displays the Group Field Collection Editor window, 
used to add and remove grouping fields, define a data field to be used as a grouping criteria and the grouping 
order (ascending or descending). 
The property is also accessible via the band's Smart tag. 
Group Union 
Determines if group rows can be printed on different pages (for both Group Header and Group Footer band the 
default is None), or the entire group is printed on a single page (if Reporting 324). 
If a group can be split across pages, but Group Headers are not to be printed on a page if there is no data row 
below it (in case a group starts at the bottom of the page and there is enough room for only a Group Header), 
set the Group Union to With First Detail. In this case, if a Group Header is alone on a page, it is moved to the 
beginning of the next page. 
The option can be applied to Group Footers. To prevent them from being printed alone on a page, set the Group 
Union to With Last Detail. The last group row from the previous page is moved to the next page and printed 
together with the Group Footer. 
The property is also accessible via the band's Smart tag. 
Keep Together 
Specifies whether the contents of the band can be horizontally split across pages. Reporting attempts to keep 
band entries on a single page. In general, if the contents are too large to fit on a single page, then the band is 
started on a new page, and continues on the following page. 
Level 
AVEVA™ Engineering
Reporting
© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
Page 4916
Specifies the nesting level of a group band. The lower the level number value, the closer the group band is to the 
Detail band. The lowest setting is zero. This parameter identifies the Group Header / Group Footer band pairs. 
The property is also accessible via the band's Smart tag. 
Page Break 
Use this property if the current report design requires that the detail section must be separated from previous 
sections or follow-ups. Specify the Before the Band or After the Band values to insert a page break before or 
after the current band. In many cases, this property can be used instead of the Page Break control. 
Print at Bottom 
The property is available for the Group Footer band only. It determines if the band is printed at the bottom of a 
page, or immediately after the last group's details. 
If a report contains several Group Footers with their Print at Bottom properties set to different values, then the 
outer Group Footer has the highest priority. 
The feature is helpful when the Page Break property of the Group Header band is set to After the Band. In this 
instance, each group starts a new page, and the Group Footer has two distinct places to reside - after the last 
row, or at the bottom of the page. 
Repeat Every Page 
Improves the readability of reports with group contents that are several pages long. Without a repeated group 
header at the top of the page, the report can be difficult to read. 
This property is also accessible via the band's Smart tag. 
Scripts 
Contains events which can be handled with scripts. 
Sorting Summary 
Displays the Group Sorting Summary Editor window, used to specify the sorting options for report groups. 
Visible 
Specifies whether the band is visible when using the print preview.

Tag 
Adds additional information to a report, for example, the id, by which the report can then be accessible via 
scripts. 
Miscellaneous 
(Name) 
Determines the name of the report, by which it can be accessed in the Report Explorer, Properties window or 
via scripts. 
Snap Line Padding 
Specifies indent values used to render the contents of the band, which are maintained when the control is 
aligned using snap lines. 
Sub-Bands 
Displays the Sub-Band Collection Editor window, which is used to manage the report sub-bands.

Report Header and Footer Bands 
The Report Header and Report Footer are the only types of report bands rendered once per report. 
The Report Header is the first band of a report on the first page, not counting margins as they are 'out-of-page' 
zones. The Report Header precedes even the Page Header band. It is the best place for the report name, 
company logo, and miscellaneous information. If a chart that visualises the report data is to be included, then 
drop the Chart control onto this band. 
The Report Footer finalises the informative part of the report. It is placed before the Page Footer and Bottom 
Margin bands on the last page of the report. Use the Report Footer for report grand totals, conclusions or other 
miscellaneous information. 
On the Properties window, the Report Header/Footer Band properties are divided into the following tabs: 
Appearance 
Background Color 
Specifies the background colour of controls. The functionality is also available on the Report Designer tab (
). 
Border Color, Border Dash Style, Border Width and Borders 
Specifies border settings for controls. 
Font 
Specifies the font settings for controls. Some of the functionality is available on the Report Designer tab. 
Foreground Color
